topics: and codes in design. Materials and their properties-Elastic and plastic behaviour of metals
stress concentration factor. Theories of Failure- Guest’s Theory
and Von Mises and Hencky Theory. Shock and impact loads
design for static and fatigue loads
reversed bending. Suggestions-problem project- Students can explore innovative approaches to design processes
and codes into practical design solutions
Rolling contact bearing- Design of bearings
dynamic equivalent load. Sliding contact bearing- lubrication
stresses and deflection of helical springs
end construction. Leaf springs- Flat springs

design of leaf springs. Suggestion-problem project -students will have the autonomy to explore and design bearing and spring systems based on specified performance criteria and real-world applications. The project will integrate concepts from rolling contact bearings
virtual or formative number of teeth

heat dissipation - lubrication of gears - Merits and demerits of each type of gears. Design of spur gear and helical gear. Suggestion- discipline project- students will explore the design and analysis of gear systems
focusing on both spur gears and helical gears. The project will involve studying gear classification
and gear failure modes. Students will apply theoretical concepts such as the Law of Gearing
Internal expanding shoe brake. Design of Internal combustion engine parts- Piston
Connecting rod and Flywheel. Suggestion - discipline project- students will explore various aspects of clutch systems
and the design of internal combustion engine parts. The project will involve both theoretical exploration and practical application
aligning with the discipline Project model where students have the freedom to choose specific aspects to explore within the defined theme
